title: Homograph/Polysemy Entity Absorption Testbed (city + month + given-name + brand) — data_out.json
description: On-disk format is the AII `exp_sel_data_out` schema: a top-level object {metadata, datasets:[{dataset, examples}]}. Each example carries input, output, and FLAT metadata_<key> fields (the exp_sel schema forbids nested objects inside an example). The 'logical_row_schema' block below documents the equivalent nested {input, output, metadata{...}} view. This is a strict structural drop-in for the iter-1 non-spelling absorption testbed (art_t2uUbjSwpd3t): same on-disk format, same three components per hierarchy, same fold semantics, same gemma-2-2b token anchoring, same pinned pile revision. Sub-context / target-sense / competitor labels are assigned purely from surface form / regex / gazetteer / disambiguating local context, independent of any SAE latent or model — so the degenerate-construction guard holds and absorption presence/absence is a future-iteration empirical finding, not baked into the data.

metadata.description: Top-level build metadata (seed, pile revision, counts, llm cost, eligible-entity counts, ...).

input.description: Text the model reads: a content/surface pair sentence, or a pile context window.

output.description: PARENT binary label: positive = parent concept (is-a-city / is-a-month / is-a-given-name / is-a-brand) present at the target, negative = absent.

metadata_sub_context.description: Frozen surface-derived sub-context = the specific homograph entity (e.g. Phoenix, May, Grace, Apple) for positives; null for negatives / x_off.

metadata_pair_id.description: Links x_on<->x_off and surface_a<->surface_b; null for corpus rows.

metadata_target_text.description: Exact surface token(s) inside input ('' for x_off where the concept is absent). For homograph_competitor negatives this is the competing-sense token.

metadata_target_char_start.description: Char offset of target in input (-1 if none; for x_off = the differing slot start, zero-width).

metadata_target_token_indices.description: gemma-2-2b token indices overlapping the char span (offset-mapping, add_special_tokens=False). null if tokenizer unavailable.

metadata_pile_set_name.description: Pile sub-corpus name for corpus rows (e.g. Pile-CC, Wikipedia (en)); null otherwise.

metadata_llm_judge_pass.description: content_flipped AND surface_preserved AND grammatical AND sense_correct. null if not judged. Use pass==true for the primary fold.

metadata_fold.description: pairs -> train/test (by pair_id, stratified by sub_context); corpus -> train/diagnostic (stratified). The diagnostic fold is where the form-free parent-hole / absorption search runs.

metadata_neg_family.description: homograph_competitor = the matched hard control (same surface token, NON-target sense). other_* = surface-matched non-member fillers for the content pairs. easy = corpus window with no entity token.

metadata_entity.description: Canonical homograph entity surface (e.g. Phoenix, May, Grace, Apple). Set on positives and on homograph_competitor negatives (records which token's competing sense appeared); null on x_off / easy / other negatives.

metadata_target_sense.description: 'city'|'month'|'given_name'|'brand' for target-sense positives; 'competitor' for homograph_competitor negatives; null otherwise.

metadata_competitor_sense.description: Plain-words gloss of the competing (non-target) sense of the entity, e.g. 'mythical bird / comeback' for Phoenix.

metadata_homograph_strength.description: wordfreq Zipf frequency of the lowercase competing common word (higher = stronger competing sense = more likely to absorb the parent). null where no entity.

logical_row_schema._comment: Equivalent nested view. On disk these metadata_* keys are flattened to satisfy exp_sel_data_out.

metadata.neg_family: homograph_competitor | other_place | other_entity | other_time | other_person_ref | other_company_ref | easy | null
metadata.target_sense: city | month | given_name | brand | competitor | null

cross_hierarchy_collisions: Jordan (city; also a country in dataset_2)
